spec_match.rationale: Pairs Reporting-Endpoints header config with in-page ReportingObserver — exactly the two surfaces the v137 stack augmentation touches.

interactive.rationale: Buttons drive a synthetic crash, the observer logs it live, side-by-side rendering of the configured endpoint payload.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises server-side config + observer mirror + synthesised payload; page does that.

edge_cases.rationale: Doesn't show what happens when the Reporting-Endpoints header is absent (does the observer still see crashes?) or when the endpoint URL is cross-origin.

design_system.rationale: Most styling via variables, but one inline style uses raw `#999` (`border-top:1px dashed #999`). Replace with var(--border-black) or var(--text-muted) per CLAUDE.md §5.

openQuestions.title: Replace the raw #999 with a token
openQuestions.detail: The block style `border-top:1px dashed #999` in the script bypasses the design system. Switch to `var(--border-black)` or `var(--text-muted)` and check WCAG AA contrast on the resulting line.

openQuestions.title: Add a no-endpoint baseline lane
openQuestions.detail: When Reporting-Endpoints is not configured, the in-page ReportingObserver still sees the event. Add a `disable endpoint` toggle to make that explicit.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate the cross-origin endpoint case
openQuestions.detail: If the configured endpoint is on a different origin, the report still ships but the spec requires CORS preflight on the POST. Show this flow with a fetch trace.

summary: Excellent pairing of header config and observer; only design-system regression is one inline #999 hex that should be a token.
